finding: A one-deployment concurrency proof establishes bounded atomicity but does not establish that Durable Object reservation state remains observable after Worker code redeployment. Pages publication should therefore be gated on a same-key replay proof across an identical-source redeployment.

implementation.ordering_invariant: worker identity -> live atomicity -> create reservation -> redeploy identical Worker source -> verify same reservation is replayed -> deploy Pages -> verify served Pages identity

verification.source_review: The committed test covers valid persistence, second-winner rejection, changed reservation identity, end-to-end before/after proof construction, and fail-closed behavior when the initial reservation is not created.
verification.workflow_run: No GitHub Actions workflow run was associated with commit 25e4181d0cb243d702c5a7cd69d3860d46f5739b at inspection time.
verification.local_execution: Unavailable in this run because the execution container could not resolve github.com to clone the public repository.
verification.live_cloudflare_execution: Unavailable until the cloudflare-research environment has valid CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID and CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN secrets and the workflow is dispatched.

evidence_strength: committed implementation and adversarial test source; live deployment unverified
limits: The proof covers one reservation key and one identical-source redeployment path.
It does not prove indefinite durability, disaster recovery, every namespace, or global availability.
Infrastructure evidence does not establish scientific or medical truth.
falsification_route: Dispatch cloudflare-research-stack.yml. The run must create one reservation before the second Worker deployment and receive a 409 replay response with the same reservation_id afterward. Any second 201, changed reservation_id, missing atomic flag, provider mismatch, or absent proof artifact falsifies acceptance.
